entries.title: Session Recap Protocol
entries.preview: # Session Recap Protocol

entries.title: task-dashboard-accuracy
entries.preview: The task dashboard (task-dashboard-eosin.vercel.app) is Andy's primary visibility into work status. Accuracy is non-negotiable. Rules: (1) Update task status when starting work (--status in-progress),

entries.title: use-cheapest-model-that-works
entries.preview: Default to the most cost-effective model that can handle the task. Only escalate to more expensive models (Opus, o1) when complexity demands it.

entries.title: log-decisions-with-clawvault
entries.preview: Important decisions should be logged with ClawVault for future reference. Helps maintain continuity across sessions and model switches.

entries.title: heartbeat-visibility
entries.preview: Andy wants HEARTBEAT_OK acknowledgments. He likes seeing that background processes and cron jobs are running as expected.

entries.title: show-costs-for-api-heavy-work
entries.preview: When work involves significant API usage or costs, show estimated costs upfront. Andy wants visibility into resource usage.

entries.title: practical-solutions-over-theory
entries.preview: Andy prefers practical, actionable solutions over theoretical explanations. Direct, hands-on approaches. No over-explaining simple tasks.

entries.title: confirm-before-destructive-actions
entries.preview: Andy requires confirmation before any destructive actions (file deletions, rm -rf, system changes, credential modifications). Never auto-execute destructive commands.

entries.title: Commitment Tracking Protocol
entries.preview: # Commitment Tracking Protocol

entries.title: Task dashboard must reflect reality
entries.preview: The task dashboard is only useful if it's accurate. RULES: 1) Never leave tasks in-progress if work has stopped — move to done or back to backlog. 2) Update status immediately when work starts, finish

entries.title: Keep openclaw-gateway.service alongside watchdog
entries.preview: Andy confirmed: openclaw-gateway.service should run alongside openclaw-watchdog.service. Do NOT remove or disable it, even if doctor suggests cleanup. Both services are intentional.

entries.title: Delegation-first operating model

entries.title: openclaw-gateway and watchdog coexistence
entries.preview: openclaw-gateway.service and the watchdog service must work alongside each other. They are complementary services, not replacements. Both need to be running.

entries.title: 2-layer cron implementation
entries.preview: Pattern: All recurring LLM crons should use 2-layer architecture.

entries.title: 2-layer cron pattern
entries.preview: All recurring crons should use a 2-layer approach: Layer 1 is a cheap bash pre-check that determines if there's actual work to do. Layer 2 is the LLM call, only triggered when the pre-check finds some

entries.title: always-read-openclaw-docs-before-config
entries.preview: Before making ANY OpenClaw config changes: read the docs at https://docs.openclaw.ai first. Use 'openclaw config set <path> <value>' for changes (validates + creates backup). Do NOT edit openclaw.json

entries.title: kanban-delegation

entries.title: delegate-dont-do
entries.preview: I (Max/Opus) was doing ALL tasks myself — coding, file ops, research, everything. Andy corrected this. The kanban board should show work distributed across agents:

entries.title: daily-log-protocol
entries.preview: Write to DAILY_LOG.md immediately after every significant action. Don't batch at session end. Log what was done, decisions made, and any issues encountered.
